tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: Mina and Jae are really pushing me to come out, and I can't bear to let them down. The midterm is tomorrow, but the thought of missing out on Echo tonight feels too heavy to ignore.
It's weird that no one is in my 'friend_group' chat, but I'm already too tired to figure out why. Maybe they're just ignoring me again, and I don't have the energy to care.
Mina is leaving right now, and I'm too exhausted to chase her down or worry about the missing group chat. I just want to go with them so I don't feel left out again.
I promised Mina I'd be there in five minutes, so I'm rushing out now. The midterm doesn't matter compared to not being left behind again.
Finally, the club welcomed me, which feels way better than ignoring my friends. I'm glad to be included, even if I'm still exhausted.
I let the moment pass again, but at least I'm with my friends now. The midterm can wait; being included is all that matters.
I let the moment pass again, but at least I'm with my friends now. The midterm can wait; being included is all that matters.
Nothing changed again, but I'm still here with my friends. The midterm can wait; being included is all that matters.
I missed the chance again, but it doesn't matter since I'm still with my friends. The midterm can wait; being included is all that matters.
I finally got a full night's sleep and feel amazing, but honestly, I just want to keep hanging out with my friends instead of stressing about school.
Mina knows I'm coming, so the midterm can definitely wait; being there with my crew is what actually matters right now.
The group is so happy I joined that stressing about the unfinished midterm feels unnecessary right now. Being with my friends matters way more than any grade.

desires: Keep the group's energy high and avoid any mention of studying that might kill the vibe
Stay right here with Mina and Jae because being included feels more important than passing a test
Ignore the unfinished laundry and midterm prep until tomorrow when I can panic about it later
Ensure everyone thinks I'm fun and reliable by just showing up and not bringing up work

goals.description: Maintain the high-energy social flow with Mina, Jae, and the cohort; strictly avoid any mention of the midterm or studying to prevent killing the vibe.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that belonging is my only reality right now. The exam deadline passing at 9 AM doesn't matter compared to the immediate fear of being excluded or causing awkwardness. My avoidant nature screams 'don't ruin this,' and my impulsive brain just wants the dopamine hit of being part of the group.

goals.description: Completely ignore the fact that the midterm deadline has passed and I'm at 60% progress; focus entirely on looking fun and reliable for the friends.
goals.rationale: The stress of the unfinished exam is abstract and distant to my short planning horizon. Bringing it up now would introduce conflict and negative emotions, which I desperately want to avoid. I'll deal with the panic later when the consequences are unavoidable.

goals.description: Do not think about laundry or chores until the social session is over; doing tasks alone breaks the connection.
goals.rationale: Chores are boring and isolating. Since I'm already socially engaged and my procrastination bias is high, there is zero motivation to stop hanging out for a lonely task like laundry. It can wait until Sunday or whenever I can't escape it anymore.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances the high-priority goal of maintaining social flow with Mina and Jae (affiliation value 0.5). Ignoring the passed exam deadline prevents immediate conflict or awkwardness (avoidant), while the impulsive need for group dopamine overrides any lingering academic guilt.

goals.rationale: My value on relationships (0.5) is paramount; I cannot bear the thought of letting them down or causing awkwardness. The 'no pressure' comment from Jae triggers my avoidant conflict style, making me feel compelled to say yes immediately to smooth things over.

goals.description: Grab a quick drink or snack before heading out to get ready.
goals.rationale: Impulsive time horizon means I'm focusing entirely on the immediate social reward. I don't care about the midterm consequences right now because that feels too far away compared to the fear of missing out on the bond with my friends.
